notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3459:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3346:### 5. Conservative queue progression
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3460:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3358:The implementation order should follow the governing workflow and the queued design decisions:
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3461:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3360:1. define the V1 review gate

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3464:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3416:### Richer status and queue models
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3465:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3440:* aggregated queue reports

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3467:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3465:5. defer broader queue features until the single-run slice has been used enough to reveal real needs
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3468:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3504:- `runner` -> literal `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3469:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3605:I’ve identified the main implementation seams already: one is documentation drift around the runner target and identity rules, and another is that the review gate exists operationally but not yet as an explicit queue-release mechanism. I’m pulling exact line references now so the report can stay grounded in the repo rather than general commentary.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3471:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3624:    16	RUNNER_PATH = "tools/codex/run_prompt.py"
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3472:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4045:     5	Define the smallest change set for [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py) so the current bridge runner writes a V1 execution record in `notes/` without a broader refactor.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3473:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4057:    17	This spec covers only the current bridge runner at [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3474:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4082:    42	The current gap is that the note conflates execution completion with outcome labeling and does not initialize the V1 review gate.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3475:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4157:   117	No broader token accounting, file-diff analysis, validation harvesting, or queue-state calculation should be added in this pass.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3480:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4328:   173	* a generalized queue engine

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3483:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4388:   233	The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3485:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4555:    12	- runner: `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3486:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4719:+- execute one prompt through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3487:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4730:+- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3488:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4731:+- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3489:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4732:+- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3490:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4733:+- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3491:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4734:+- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3492:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4741:+- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3493:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4757:+### 1. Conservative queue progression is still architectural, not operational

See [V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md:37) versus [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:59).

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3516:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5039:+  - A small test or fixture harness around `run_prompt.py` and `review_run.py` that locks in the record shape, allowed review transitions, and in-place write-back behavior.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3518:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5108:+### 1. Add a queue-readiness checker

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3520:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5134:+The next step should be: `Add a queue-readiness checker`.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3521:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5162:+1. queue-readiness checker
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3522:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5181:+- execute one prompt through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3523:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5192:+- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3524:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5193:+- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3525:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5194:+- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3526:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5195:+- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3527:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5196:+- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3528:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5203:+- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3529:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5219:+### 1. Conservative queue progression is still architectural, not operational
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3530:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5241:+The execution-record artifact says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `run_prompt.py` adds `__2`, `__3`, etc. on same-second collisions. See [V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md:37) versus [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:59).

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3582:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5976:+- execute one prompt through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3583:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5987:+- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3584:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5988:+- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3585:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5989:+- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3586:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5990:+- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3587:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5991:+- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3588:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5998:+- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3589:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6014:+### 1. Conservative queue progression is still architectural, not operational
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3590:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6036:+The execution-record artifact says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `run_prompt.py` adds `__2`, `__3`, etc. on same-second collisions. See [V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md:37) versus [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:59).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3591:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6042:+- `tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md` still targets `baby_run_prompt.py` as the implementation target and still names the runner as `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`. See [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:5), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:17), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:156), and [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:252).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3592:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6043:+- The design packet now implicitly assumes a review write-back step, but the architecture document still describes conservative queue progression as part of the V1 shape without a concrete implementation artifact for that responsibility. See [Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:161) and [Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:215).

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3618:notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6430:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. The top recommendation is to add a small queue-readiness checker next, followed by an unreviewed-run lister and then lightweight contract validation for the two V1 scripts.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3660:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:572:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3661:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:998:`tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3662:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1019:- The runner does one bounded thing: resolve a prompt, execute `codex exec`, and write a local note. That fits the workflow's allowance for thin bridge tooling that gathers evidence while remaining inspectable and reversible ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:114), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:99)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3663:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1020:- It validates basic directory prerequisites and fails cleanly when expected folders or prompt files are missing, which is a reasonable minimal safeguard for a bridge script ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:109), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:108)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3664:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1021:- It writes timestamped markdown notes into `notes/`, which gives the system at least some durable local history of what was run and what Codex returned ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:46), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:135)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3665:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1025:- Prompt resolution policy is embedded in the runner. Selection by absolute path, repo-relative path, `codex_prompts/` path, or globbed prefix is helpful now, but prompt identity and lookup rules belong in a prompt-discovery/status layer if the system grows ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:47), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:25)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3666:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1026:- Note-path and note-content policy are also embedded here. That is acceptable for a bridge script, but note schema, status vocabulary, and execution-record structure should eventually be owned by a note/history module rather than the process launcher ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:96), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:50)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3667:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1027:- The runner currently decides final state as `SUCCESS` or `FAILED` based only on the subprocess exit code. That is an expedient bridge behavior, but review judgment, validation outcome, and richer state modeling should not stay inside the launcher if the workflow matures ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:124)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3668:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1031:- As bridge tooling it is still acceptable, but it is only partially subordinate to workflow. It captures execution, yet it does not encode the review point that should separate execution from acceptance. A zero exit code becomes `SUCCESS` immediately, which skips the workflow's "validate and inspect before the next step" posture ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:271), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:125)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3669:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1032:- It stores the original prompt text and final output, but not explicit success criteria, validation evidence, or reviewer judgment. That means the note is an execution artifact, not yet a full workflow artifact ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3670:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1036:- The current setup provides some raw failure evidence because nonzero exit runs still write a note and stderr is preserved when present ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:128)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3671:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1042:- The setup preserves only minimal cost evidence today: timestamp in the note filename/content and the captured final output/stderr. That gives some weak signal about artifact volume, but almost nothing about runtime, retries, or service usage ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:17), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3672:notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1085:I have the governing constraints and the main seam: the current bridge runner records execution, but it conflates execution success with reviewed acceptance and leaves the run artifact underdefined. I’m checking the existing prompt queue so the next sequence stays small and fits the repository’s current naming pattern.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3942:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:293:- `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3945:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:359:- compute queue state
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3946:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:364:### 2. Respect the review gate
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3947:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:434:6. `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` remains unchanged
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3948:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:435:7. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` remains unchanged
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3949:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:445:5. Do not build broader queue or workflow logic.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3950:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:454:- it writes back review facts consistent with the V1 review gate
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3951:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:460:I’m inspecting the specified workflow docs, runner files, and sample notes first so the helper matches the existing V1 record shape and review gate without touching the runners.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3952:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:514:* one manual review gate
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3953:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:544:* stopping the queue at `UNREVIEWED`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3954:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:557:* richer queue state machines
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3955:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:607:### 4. V1 review gate definition
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3956:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:621:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3957:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:639:* a generalized queue engine
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3958:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:677:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3959:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:688:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3960:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:699:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3961:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:775:`005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__20260415_202557`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3962:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:782:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3963:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1214:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3964:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1220:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3965:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1237:- a larger queue state machine
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3966:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1283:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3967:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1295:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3968:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1297:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3969:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1299:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3970:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1300:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3971:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1304:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3972:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1315:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3973:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1345:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3974:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1352:- automatic queue release
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3975:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1354:- separate queue-level state files
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3976:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1362:The minimum V1 review gate is:
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3977:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1373:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/run_prompt.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3994:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2022:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' notes/009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3995:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2028:Built `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` as a thin V1 runner candidate that resolves one prompt, runs `codex exec` from the repo root, captures `--output-last-message`, captures stderr and return code, writes one V1 execution-record markdown file into `notes/`, prints that path, and exits with the subprocess return code.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3996:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2030:## Preserved From `baby_run_prompt.py`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3997:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2054:- Updated the runner identity field to `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3998:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2082:- `python3 tools/codex/run_prompt.py codex_prompts/001_smoke_test_pipeline.md`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:3999:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2091:- `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` remained unchanged. `git diff -- tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` returned no diff.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4000:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2099:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4001:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2105:`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. The sample record is structurally close to the V1 artifact shape and preserves the failure evidence that matters. The most important next issue is environment-related, not runner design or broader implementation.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4002:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2109:### Does `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` successfully implement the V1 design intent?
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4003:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2121:- no queue automation or review automation
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4004:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2125:- It stays thin and preserves the bounded behavior of `baby_run_prompt.py` instead of turning into a larger system.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4005:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2136:- The runner identity now correctly points at `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, but `tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md` still describes the prior target as `baby_run_prompt.py`. That is a doc drift issue more than a runner issue.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4006:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2207:- Nothing in `run_prompt.py` suggests it is forcing a read-only mode or attempting forbidden writes beyond the existing thin subprocess path.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4007:notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2246:A short diagnosis artifact that identifies where session startup is trying to write, whether the failure reproduces independently of `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, and one bounded follow-up action.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4046:notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:11:| 05 – SVMs | ~0% | None (heading only) | Entire notebook (60 code cells) | Do last |
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4047:notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:21:Zero Goal Before / Implementation Notes cells. The 60 non-empty code cells have no pedagogical treatment whatsoever. What exists is purely the original Géron narrative — section headings, brief book-prose intros to subsections, and exercise solutions with conversational narration — none of it in the Goal/Implementation format.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4048:notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:25:Cell 0 is just `**Support Vector Machines**` — 27 characters. There is no chapter-level intro in the treatment format. Not even close.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4049:notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:29:The entire notebook is untreated. Every code cell from cell 5 through cell 112 (60 code cells) lacks both a Goal Before and an Implementation Notes After cell. This isn't a gap — it's a blank canvas.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4050:notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:33:This notebook needs a complete treatment pass from scratch. Significant work.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4051:notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:41:All 58 non-empty code cells have both a "### Goal Before This Cell" and a "### Implementation Notes After This Cell" cell. The treatment is thorough, consistent in format, and well-written throughout. The 5% gap is one thing: the chapter-level intro.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4052:notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:45:Cell 0: `**Chapter 6 – Decision Trees**` — 30 characters. Just a bold heading. There is no chapter-level intro in the treatment format — none at all. This is the single missing piece.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4053:notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:49:No untreated code cell stretches. The only gap is the chapter-level intro markdown cell that should appear near the top of the notebook before the Setup section. Everything else is done.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4054:notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:61:Only 17 of 73 non-empty code cells have both a Goal + Implementation treatment. The notebook uses a different and inconsistent treatment vocabulary — cells say "**Why run this cell**", "**Result**:", "**What it is**" instead of the canonical "### Goal Before This Cell" / "### Implementation Notes After This Cell" format. So even treated cells may not fully match the template.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4055:notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:73:Substantial work remaining. The treatment that exists uses a looser format than the canonical template. A full pass would need to both add missing cells AND potentially revise existing partial cells to use the standard structure.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4056:notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:81:NB07 is second priority — has a foundation but needs a major treatment pass.  
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4057:notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:82:NB05 should be scheduled last — needs a full treatment from scratch.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4058:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:14:Your task is to define the smallest change spec for `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` so it can support the V1 execution record model without a larger refactor.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4062:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:56:6. What current behaviors in `baby_run_prompt.py` should remain unchanged for V1?

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4065:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:130:- `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4066:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:132:- `notes/005_prompt_queue_plan__20260415_202557.md`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4067:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:136:Produce a narrow implementation spec for the current bridge runner.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4068:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:162:6. What current behaviors in `baby_run_prompt.py` should remain unchanged for V1?
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4069:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:193:- the spec is directly grounded in the V1 execution record and review gate
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4070:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:199:  → Read the specified workflow, architecture, V1 record, review gate, runner, and note files
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4071:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:476:* one manual review gate
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4072:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:506:* stopping the queue at `UNREVIEWED`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4073:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:519:* richer queue state machines
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4074:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:569:### 4. V1 review gate definition
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4075:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:583:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4076:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:601:* a generalized queue engine
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4077:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:639:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4078:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:650:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4079:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:661:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4080:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:737:`005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__20260415_202557`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4081:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:744:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4082:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:952:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4083:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:958:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4084:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:975:- a larger queue state machine
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4085:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1021:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4086:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1033:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4087:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1035:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4088:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1037:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4089:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1038:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4090:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1042:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4091:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1053:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4092:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1083:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4093:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1090:- automatic queue release
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4094:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1092:- separate queue-level state files
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4095:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1100:The minimum V1 review gate is:
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4096:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1111:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4097:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1273:`tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4098:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1294:- The runner does one bounded thing: resolve a prompt, execute `codex exec`, and write a local note. That fits the workflow's allowance for thin bridge tooling that gathers evidence while remaining inspectable and reversible ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:114), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:99)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4099:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1295:- It validates basic directory prerequisites and fails cleanly when expected folders or prompt files are missing, which is a reasonable minimal safeguard for a bridge script ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:109), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:108)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4100:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1296:- It writes timestamped markdown notes into `notes/`, which gives the system at least some durable local history of what was run and what Codex returned ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:46), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:135)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4101:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1300:- Prompt resolution policy is embedded in the runner. Selection by absolute path, repo-relative path, `codex_prompts/` path, or globbed prefix is helpful now, but prompt identity and lookup rules belong in a prompt-discovery/status layer if the system grows ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:47), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:25)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4102:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1301:- Note-path and note-content policy are also embedded here. That is acceptable for a bridge script, but note schema, status vocabulary, and execution-record structure should eventually be owned by a note/history module rather than the process launcher ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:96), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:50)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4103:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1302:- The runner currently decides final state as `SUCCESS` or `FAILED` based only on the subprocess exit code. That is an expedient bridge behavior, but review judgment, validation outcome, and richer state modeling should not stay inside the launcher if the workflow matures ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:124)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4104:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1306:- As bridge tooling it is still acceptable, but it is only partially subordinate to workflow. It captures execution, yet it does not encode the review point that should separate execution from acceptance. A zero exit code becomes `SUCCESS` immediately, which skips the workflow's "validate and inspect before the next step" posture ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:271), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:125)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4105:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1307:- It stores the original prompt text and final output, but not explicit success criteria, validation evidence, or reviewer judgment. That means the note is an execution artifact, not yet a full workflow artifact ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4106:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1311:- The current setup provides some raw failure evidence because nonzero exit runs still write a note and stderr is preserved when present ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:128)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4107:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1317:- The setup preserves only minimal cost evidence today: timestamp in the note filename/content and the captured final output/stderr. That gives some weak signal about artifact volume, but almost nothing about runtime, retries, or service usage ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:17), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4108:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1335:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' notes/005_prompt_queue_plan__20260415_202557.md" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4109:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1344:The queue stays small on purpose.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4110:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1351:1. define the review gate
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4111:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1355:This reduces the risk of hard-coding the wrong note model or the wrong queue semantics into the runner before the manual review loop is settled.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4112:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1373:The following work is intentionally not in this queue:
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4113:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1473:### 5. Conservative queue progression
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4114:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1485:The implementation order should follow the governing workflow and the queued design decisions:
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4115:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1487:1. define the V1 review gate
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4116:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1488:2. align the architecture document to the workflow, the execution record, and the review gate
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4117:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1498:* coupling queue progression to process exit
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4118:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1543:### Richer status and queue models
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4119:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1567:* aggregated queue reports
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4120:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1589:2. keep the review gate explicit
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:4121:notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1592:5. defer broader queue features until the single-run slice has been used enough to reveal real needs

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:5915:notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7460:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:10012:+    base_run_id = f"{prompt_stem}__{started_at_utc}"
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:5916:notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7461:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:10013:+    if run_id == base_run_id:
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:5917:notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7462:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:10016:+    suffix_match = re.fullmatch(rf"{re.escape(base_run_id)}__(\d+)", run_id)
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:5918:notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7463:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:10018:+        raise ReadinessError(f"run_id does not match V1 identity pattern: {run_id}")
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:5919:notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7464:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:10047:+    run_id = values["run_id"] or ""
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:5920:notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7465:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:10073:+        run_id=run_id,
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:5921:notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7466:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:10079:+        run_suffix=parse_run_suffix(prompt_stem, started_at_utc, run_id),
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:5922:notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7467:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:10123:+def evaluate_readiness(
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:5923:notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7468:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:10202:+        result = evaluate_readiness(prompts, records, target)
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:5924:notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7469:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:10213:I found the last false positive source: real V1 records keep `execution_status` and `review_status` after the first section break, while legacy notes don’t carry the V1 `run_id` header at all. I’m switching the parser to use the header only to decide “is this a V1 record candidate?”, then reading the full metadata block up to `## Prompt Text` for the required fields.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:5926:notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7471:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:10217:diff --git a/notes/014_queue_readiness_checker_build__20260416_002419.md b/notes/014_queue_readiness_checker_build__20260416_002419.md

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6271:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:167:The report classifies `notes/` and `codex_prompts/` into the four requested buckets, with file-specific reasons. The main conservative call is that current V1 execution records remain `uncertain` because `check_queue_readiness.py` and `list_review_backlog.py` still treat them as live source-of-truth data, so moving them now would change behavior. No files were moved or deleted.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6275:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:389:* stopping the queue at `UNREVIEWED`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6276:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:402:* richer queue state machines
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6277:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:452:### 4. V1 review gate definition
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6278:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:466:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6279:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:472:* `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` for bounded execution and execution-record creation
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6280:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:474:* `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` for conservative next-prompt readiness checks
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6281:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:496:* a generalized queue engine
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6282:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:534:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6283:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:545:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6284:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:556:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6309:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:935:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6310:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:937:`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` uses that base identity and adds a numeric suffix such as `__2` only when needed to avoid a same-second filename collision for the same prompt.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6311:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1112:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6312:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1118:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6313:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1135:- a larger queue state machine
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6314:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1181:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6315:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1193:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6316:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1195:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6317:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1197:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6318:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1198:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6319:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1202:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6320:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1213:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6321:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1243:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6322:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1250:- automatic queue release
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6323:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1252:- separate queue-level state files

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6492:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4533:- `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6493:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4535:- `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6494:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4925:`tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6495:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4946:- The runner does one bounded thing: resolve a prompt, execute `codex exec`, and write a local note. That fits the workflow's allowance for thin bridge tooling that gathers evidence while remaining inspectable and reversible ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:114), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:99)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6496:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4947:- It validates basic directory prerequisites and fails cleanly when expected folders or prompt files are missing, which is a reasonable minimal safeguard for a bridge script ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:109), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:108)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6497:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4948:- It writes timestamped markdown notes into `notes/`, which gives the system at least some durable local history of what was run and what Codex returned ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:46), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:135)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6498:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4952:- Prompt resolution policy is embedded in the runner. Selection by absolute path, repo-relative path, `codex_prompts/` path, or globbed prefix is helpful now, but prompt identity and lookup rules belong in a prompt-discovery/status layer if the system grows ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:47), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:25)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6499:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4953:- Note-path and note-content policy are also embedded here. That is acceptable for a bridge script, but note schema, status vocabulary, and execution-record structure should eventually be owned by a note/history module rather than the process launcher ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:96), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:50)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6500:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4954:- The runner currently decides final state as `SUCCESS` or `FAILED` based only on the subprocess exit code. That is an expedient bridge behavior, but review judgment, validation outcome, and richer state modeling should not stay inside the launcher if the workflow matures ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:124)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6501:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4958:- As bridge tooling it is still acceptable, but it is only partially subordinate to workflow. It captures execution, yet it does not encode the review point that should separate execution from acceptance. A zero exit code becomes `SUCCESS` immediately, which skips the workflow's "validate and inspect before the next step" posture ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:271), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:125)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6502:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4959:- It stores the original prompt text and final output, but not explicit success criteria, validation evidence, or reviewer judgment. That means the note is an execution artifact, not yet a full workflow artifact ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6503:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4963:- The current setup provides some raw failure evidence because nonzero exit runs still write a note and stderr is preserved when present ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:128)).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6504:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4969:- The setup preserves only minimal cost evidence today: timestamp in the note filename/content and the captured final output/stderr. That gives some weak signal about artifact volume, but almost nothing about runtime, retries, or service usage ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:17), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6593:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:6548:Implemented the V1 runner candidate in [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py). It keeps the bootstrap runner’s prompt resolution and thin `codex exec` subprocess flow, but now writes a single V1 execution-record markdown artifact with separate execution and review sections, blank review/failure-analysis fields, lightweight resource metrics, stable run-id naming, and numeric suffix collision handling.
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6594:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:6550:I also wrote the implementation note at [009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md) and validated the runner by invoking it against the smoke-test prompt. That produced [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md), which has the required V1 shape. The `codex exec` subprocess itself exited `1` because session initialization hit `Read-only file system (os error 30)`, so the sample record correctly shows `execution_status: EXECUTION_FAILED`, `review_status: UNREVIEWED`, captured stderr, and the printed record path. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` was not modified.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6628:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:6984:`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. The sample record is structurally close to the V1 artifact shape and preserves the failure evidence that matters. The most important next issue is environment-related, not runner design or broader implementation.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6661:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7642:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. The top recommendation is to add a small queue-readiness checker next, followed by an unreviewed-run lister and then lightweight contract validation for the two V1 scripts.

notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6672:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7778:- execute one prompt through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6673:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7789:- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6674:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7790:- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6675:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7791:- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6676:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7792:- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6677:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7793:- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6678:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7800:- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6679:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7816:### 1. Conservative queue progression is still architectural, not operational
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6680:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7838:The execution-record artifact says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `run_prompt.py` adds `__2`, `__3`, etc. on same-second collisions. See [V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md:37) versus [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:59).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6681:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7844:- `tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md` still targets `baby_run_prompt.py` as the implementation target and still names the runner as `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`. See [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:5), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:17), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:156), and [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:252).
notes/023_tools_codex_assessment__20260417_171721.md:6682:notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7845:- The design packet now implicitly assumes a review write-back step, but the architecture document still describes conservative queue progression as part of the V1 shape without a concrete implementation artifact for that responsibility. See [Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:161) and [Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:215).
